expected_trades_per_day_basis: Triple-AND of three filters whose individual triggers are 3443/2157/988 over the ~30d data window; intersection conservatively ~45 events / 30d ≈ 1.5/day, matching E148's 988-trig floor as the binding constraint.

best_in: BTC mild-up to sideways with elevated alt taker-buy aggression
high global short-ratio days (crowded shorts being squeezed)
high-liquidity majors during US/EU overlap when taker_buy_ratio_5m can sustain >=0.65
worst_in: broad risk-off / BTC dump days where pump signals fail and crowded shorts are correct
low-volatility chop where taker_buy_ratio_5m oscillates around 0.5 and triggers fire on noise
funding-flip-positive regimes where the short-squeeze fuel is already spent
regime_sensitivity.explanation: All three parent components (E126, E051, E148) share pump-long geometry, so the archetype is structurally a long-squeeze hunter. It needs both directional fuel (taker buys) and crowded counter-positioning (global shorts) on a liquid book — remove any one and the asymmetric TP/SL trap from R2 dominates.

live_readiness_caveats: Triple-intersection means parent-overlap risk is high — if overlap_rate vs E126 exceeds 80% the archetype is just a thinner E126 and inherits the R2 paper trap (-13.5% on $1000 despite 80% win rate).

liquidity_bucket=high partially mitigates slippage, but taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.65 is a momentum-chasing entry — expect 5-15bps adverse fill on market orders during the squeeze impulse, which compounds against the asymmetric TP/SL.

Trigger rate of ~1.5/day means single-symbol concurrent positions are unlikely, but cluster-fires across correlated alts (e.g. mid-cap L1s squeezing together) can produce 3-5 simultaneous longs — size at 5% not 10% per trade until confirmed.

monitoring_metrics.metric: overlap_rate vs E126/E051/E148 (% of entries that also fire on any parent within ±60s)
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Synthesizer's explicit watch condition — without marginal value over parents, this is dead weight.
R2 lesson: legacy p25 hid the asymmetric tail on E126. kelly_capped is the metric that caught the trap and is the one this archetype was conditionally accepted on.
Below 0.5 means the triple-AND is over-selective and sample is too thin to estimate edge; above 4.0 means a filter (likely global_short_ratio_high) is mis-thresholded and we're back to E126 geometry.

monitoring_metrics.metric: realized median trade % vs backtest median
monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: live median <50% of backtest median for 20+ trades
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Direct paper-shadow vs backtest divergence — the canonical test for whether the asymmetric TP/SL trap is materializing live.
Asymmetric TP/SL (parent E126 was TP=0.51 SL=6.00) means each loss is ~12x a win in magnitude; 4 consecutive losses ≈ 48x one win, which is the structural failure mode.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 7-day rolling kelly_capped <0 AND realized 7-day P&L <-3% on $1000
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Combines the metric that exposed the R2 trap with a hard capital-drawdown floor — this is the exact failure mode R2 found on E126 paper.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: overlap_rate vs E126 >80% confirmed over first 50 live triggers
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: The archetype's only justification over its parents is marginal value from triple-intersection — if it's just a thinner E126, kill and trade E126 directly with the same SL discipline.

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 2 or more single-trade losses exceeding -8% (i.e. SL hit deeper than backtest assumed)
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Indicates execution slippage on the SL side is materially worse than backtest, which against an asymmetric TP/SL geometry is unrecoverable — no amount of win-rate fixes a fat left tail.

expected_max_dd_basis: Inheriting E126's asymmetric TP=0.51/SL=6.00 geometry on 5% sizing, 4 consecutive SL hits (plausible at 1.5 trades/day over a 3-day adverse regime) yields ~9% account DD; high_liq filter trims tail vs raw E126 but triple-AND selectivity does not change loss magnitude per trade.
compare_to_existing: E126 pc_pump_taker_buy_extreme_long — same pump-long geometry and the archetype this most directly extends; the R2 paper-shadow result on E126 (-13.5% on $1000 despite backtest 0.3946) is the single most relevant prior for what to expect here.
